- **Memory (Tools Used & Results):** {'Action Step 1': {'tool_name': 'Ground_Google_Search_Tool', 'sub_goal': 'Find the exact date of the gunfight at the O.K. Corral and the subsequent legal proceedings involving Thomas Fitch and John Henry Holliday.', 'command': 'execution = tool.execute(query="When was the gunfight at the O.K. Corral and the legal proceedings involving Thomas Fitch and John Henry Holliday?", add_citations=True)', 'result': ['The gunfight at the O.K. Corral occurred on October 26, 1881, in Tombstone, Arizona Territory.[1](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/), [2](https://historynet.com/ok-corral/), [3](https://www.historytoday.com/archive/months-past/gunfight-ok-corral-becomes-legend), [4](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/history/analysis-shootout-ok-corral), [5](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gunfight_at_the_O.K._Corral), [6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/), [7](http://www.lovewellhistory.com/blog/words-and-bullets.html)

The legal proceedings involving John Henry "Doc" Holliday and the Earp brothers (Wyatt, Virgil, and Morgan), with Thomas Fitch as their defense attorney, began shortly after the gunfight.[8](https://www.famous-trials.com/earp/503-home), [9](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Fitch_(politician)), [7](http://www.lovewellhistory.com/blog/words-and-bullets.html), [10](https://www.truewestmagazine.com/blog/the-surprise-witness/)

Here's a timeline of the legal proceedings:
*   **October 28, 1881:** Coroner Henry Mathews convened an inquest into the deaths of Billy Clanton, Tom McLaury, and Frank McLaury.[6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/)
*   **October 29, 1881:** Ike Clanton filed murder charges against the Earps and Doc Holliday.[8](https://www.famous-trials.com/earp/503-home), [6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/), [11](https://scholarwolf.unr.edu/bitstreams/3f6b2937-a0d5-4d33-b969-cb6d4be2b4cb/download)
*   **October 31, 1881:** Judge Wells Spicer commenced a preliminary hearing to determine if the Earps and Holliday should be tried for murder.[8](https://www.famous-trials.com/earp/503-home), [6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/)
*   **November 7, 1881:** During the hearing, Judge Wells Spicer revoked the bail of Wyatt Earp and Doc Holliday, ordering them jailed.[12](https://www.truewestmagazine.com/blog/the-jailing-of-wyatt-earp-and-doc-holliday/)
*   **November 29, 1881:** The preliminary hearing concluded.[6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/)
*   **November 30, 1881:** Judge Spicer issued his ruling, exonerating the Earps and Holliday.[6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/)']}}

Action Step 1.command: execution = tool.execute(query="When was the gunfight at the O.K. Corral and the legal proceedings involving Thomas Fitch and John Henry Holliday?", add_citations=True)
result:
The gunfight at the O.K. Corral occurred on October 26, 1881, in Tombstone, Arizona Territory.[1](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/), [2](https://historynet.com/ok-corral/), [3](https://www.historytoday.com/archive/months-past/gunfight-ok-corral-becomes-legend), [4](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/history/analysis-shootout-ok-corral), [5](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gunfight_at_the_O.K._Corral), [6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/), [7](http://www.lovewellhistory.com/blog/words-and-bullets.html)

The legal proceedings involving John Henry "Doc" Holliday and the Earp brothers (Wyatt, Virgil, and Morgan), with Thomas Fitch as their defense attorney, began shortly after the gunfight.[8](https://www.famous-trials.com/earp/503-home), [9](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Fitch_(politician)), [7](http://www.lovewellhistory.com/blog/words-and-bullets.html), [10](https://www.truewestmagazine.com/blog/the-surprise-witness/)

Here's a timeline of the legal proceedings:
*   **October 28, 1881:** Coroner Henry Mathews convened an inquest into the deaths of Billy Clanton, Tom McLaury, and Frank McLaury.[6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/)
*   **October 29, 1881:** Ike Clanton filed murder charges against the Earps and Doc Holliday.[8](https://www.famous-trials.com/earp/503-home), [6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/), [11](https://scholarwolf.unr.edu/bitstreams/3f6b2937-a0d5-4d33-b969-cb6d4be2b4cb/download)
*   **October 31, 1881:** Judge Wells Spicer commenced a preliminary hearing to determine if the Earps and Holliday should be tried for murder.[8](https://www.famous-trials.com/earp/503-home), [6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/)
*   **November 7, 1881:** During the hearing, Judge Wells Spicer revoked the bail of Wyatt Earp and Doc Holliday, ordering them jailed.[12](https://www.truewestmagazine.com/blog/the-jailing-of-wyatt-earp-and-doc-holliday/)
*   **November 29, 1881:** The preliminary hearing concluded.[6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/)
*   **November 30, 1881:** Judge Spicer issued his ruling, exonerating the Earps and Holliday.[6](https://justiceforall.ca2.uscourts.gov/reenactments/wyatt-earp/wyatt-earp-timeline/)

direct_output:
<answer>
The gunfight at the O.K. Corral occurred on October 26, 1881, in Tombstone, Arizona Territory. Thomas Fitch defended John Henry Holliday during the subsequent legal proceedings, which began shortly after the gunfight. The preliminary hearing to determine if Holliday and the Earps should be tried for murder started on October 31, 1881, and concluded on November 29, 1881, with Judge Wells Spicer exonerating them.
</answer>
